topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a grating soundtrack to my mounting anxiety. I stared at the screen, the numbers mocking me. Sarah’s salary, displayed in a crisp spreadsheet, dwarfed mine by a margin that felt impossibly wide. A tight knot formed in my chest, squeezing the air from my lungs. I picked up my lukewarm coffee mug, the ceramic suddenly feeling heavy and cold in my hands. Every time I glanced over at her desk, I felt a distinct tightening around my jaw. I avoided eye contact, focusing instead on the dust motes dancing in the afternoon sunbeam slicing through the blinds. It was a quiet, internal collapse, a sinking feeling that had nothing to do with the quarterly reports and everything to do with my own perceived inadequacy.

[story 2]

My palms were slick as I gripped the edge of my desk. When Mark showed me the compensation breakdown, the sheer disparity between his earnings and mine hit me like a physical blow. I could feel a flush creeping up my neck, hot and undeniable. I mumbled something in response, a pathetic squeak that died quickly in the open-plan office. I shifted my weight from one foot to the other, the movement jerky and uncontrolled. I wanted to disappear, to become invisible. The silence in my immediate vicinity seemed amplified, pressing in on me. I spent the next hour meticulously retyping emails, each keystroke labored, as if I were wading through thick mud, trying desperately to maintain a semblance of professional composure while my internal landscape felt utterly devastated.

[story 3]

I kept glancing toward the corner where Liam sat, his demeanor effortlessly composed. The figures he presented were undeniable, a stark reality laid bare in columns of figures. A wave of profound deflation washed over me, leaving a hollow ache in my stomach. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and let out a slow, ragged breath. My shoulders slumped forward, a visible surrender to the situation. I fiddled incessantly with the cuff of my shirt, my fingers tracing the worn fabric as if seeking some reassurance there. When Liam finally looked up, his expression was neutral, and in that moment, I felt utterly exposed, my defeat palpable in the slump of my posture.
